title: Minimal Avatar Smart Wallet (MASW)
description: A smart‚Äëwallet interface for EIP‚Äë7702 account‚Äëcode delegation.
body:
## Abstract Minimal Avatar Smart Wallet (MASW) is an immutable delegate‚Äëwallet that any EOA can designate via [EIP‚Äë7702](./eip-7702) (txType `0x04`). Once designated, the wallet's code remains active for every subsequent transaction until the owner sends a new `0x04` to clear or replace it. During each delegated call the EOA is the avatar and MASW's code executes as the delegate at the same address, enabling atomic batched calls ([EIP‚Äë712](./eip-712) signed) and optional sponsor gas reimbursement in ETH or [ERC‚Äë20](./eip-20). The contract offers one primary function, `executeBatch`, plus two plug‚Äëin hooks: a Policy Module for pre/post guards and a Recovery Module for alternate signature validation. Replay attacks are prevented by a global metaNonce, an expiry, and a chain‚Äëbound `EIP‚Äë712` domain separator. Standardising this seven‚Äëparameter ABI removes wallet fragmentation while still allowing custom logic through modules. ## Motivation A single‚Äëtransaction code‚Äëinjection model (EIP‚Äë7702) grants EOAs full implementation freedom, but unconstrained diversity would impose high coordination costs: - **Interoperability** ‚Äì Divergent ABIs and fee‚Äësettlement conventions force dApps and relayers to maintain per‚Äëwallet adapters, increasing integration complexity and failure modes. - **Economic alignment** ‚Äì Gas‚Äësponsorship relies on deterministic fee‚Äëreimbursement paths; heterogeneity erodes relayer incentives and throttles sponsored‚Äëtransaction volume. - **Tooling precision** ‚Äì Indexers, debuggers, and static‚Äëanalysis frameworks achieve optimal decoding and gas estimation when targeting a single, fixed byte‚Äëcode and seven‚Äëfield call schema. - **Extensibility focus** ‚Äì Constraining variability to two module boundaries (Policy, Recovery) localizes complexity, allowing research and hardening efforts to concentrate on higher‚Äëlevel security primitives rather than re‚Äëengineering core wallet logic. By standardising the immutable byte‚Äëcode, signature domain, and minimal ABI while exposing clearly defined extension hooks, MASW minimizes fragmentation and maximizes composability across the Ethereum tooling stack. ## Specification The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in RFC 2119 and RFC 8174. ### Overview of Delegation Flow 1. **Deploy** `MASW` with constructor argument `_owner = EOA`. 2. The owner sends an EIP‚Äë7702 transaction (txType¬Ý`0x04`) referencing the contract's byte‚Äëcode hash. 3. After that transaction the EOA acts as the **avatar wallet** while the MASW logic executes as the **delegate wallet** at the _same_ address. ### Public Interface ```solidity function executeBatch( address[] calldata targets, uint256[] calldata values, bytes[] calldata calldatas, address token, uint256 fee, uint256 expiry, bytes calldata signature ) external; function setPolicyModule(address newModule) external; function setRecoveryModule(address newModule) external; event BatchExecuted(bytes32 indexed structHash); event ModuleChanged(bytes32 indexed kind, address oldModule, address newModule); ``` ### Transaction Type Hash ```solidity bytes32 constant BATCH_TYPEHASH = keccak256( "Batch(address[] targets,uint256[] values,bytes[] calldatas,address token,uint256 fee,uint256 exp,uint256 metaNonce)" ); ``` ### Storage Layout | Slot | Name | Type | Description | | ---: | ---------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------- | | 0 | `metaNonce` | uint256 | Monotonically increasing meta‚Äënonce | | 1 | `_entered` | uint256 | Re‚Äëentrancy guard flag | | 2 | `policyModule` | address | Optional `IPolicyModule` (zero = none) | | 3 | `recoveryModule` | address | Optional `IRecoveryModule` (zero = none) | `owner` and `DOMAIN_SEPARATOR` are `immutable` and occupy no storage slots. ### Domain Separator Construction ```solidity DOMAIN_SEPARATOR = keccak256( abi.encode( keccak256("EIP712Domain(string name,string version,uint256 chainId,address verifyingContract)"), keccak256("MASW"), keccak256("1"), block.chainid, // MUST be the live chain‚ÄëID; using 0 is disallowed _owner // keeps separator stable before & after delegation ) ); ``` ### Batch Execution (`executeBatch`) | Stage | Behaviour | | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **Validation** | ‚Äë `targets.length == values.length == calldatas.length > 0`
‚Äë `block.timestamp ‚â§ expiry`
‚Äë `metaNonce` matches then increments
‚Äë `EIP712` digest recovers `owner` **or** is approved by `recoveryModule` | | **Policy¬Ýpre‚Äëhook** | If `policyModule != address(0)`, `preCheck` **MUST** return `true`; a revert or `false` vetoes the batch | | **Calls** | For each index _i_: `targets[i].call{value:values[i]}(calldatas[i])`; revert on first failure | | **Policy¬Ýpost‚Äëhook** | Same semantics as pre‚Äëhook | | **Fee reimbursement** | If `fee > 0`: native transfer (`token == address(0)`) or `ERC20` `transfer` with OpenZeppelin‚Äëstyle return‚Äëvalue check | | **Emit** | `BatchExecuted(structHash)` | #### Gas Sponsorship The relayer and owner agree off‚Äëchain on `(token, fee)` prior to submission. Because the fee is part of the signed batch, a relayer cannot unilaterally raise it. If a rival relayer broadcasts the same signed batch first, they earn the fee and the original relayer's transaction reverts‚Äîaligning incentives naturally. Relayers **MUST** confirm the avatar's balance up‚Äëfront; insufficient funds render the transaction invalid in the mem‚Äëpool. ### Modules #### Policy Module ```solidity interface IPolicyModule { function preCheck (address sender, bytes calldata rawData, uint256 value) external view returns (bool); function postCheck(address sender, bytes calldata rawData, uint256 value) external view returns (bool); } ``` - A module **MAY** veto by reverting _or_ by returning `false`. - The `value` parameter represents the total ETH sent with the transaction (`msg.value`), allowing the policy module to validate this against the batch requirements contained in `rawData`. - Aggregator designs are encouraged: forward to child policies and stop on first failure (revert or return `false`). #### Recovery Module ```solidity interface IRecoveryModule { function isValidSignature(bytes32 hash, bytes calldata sig) external view returns (bytes4); } ``` Must return `0x1626ba7e`. ### Nonce‚ÄëRace Consideration A single global `metaNonce` is used. Two relayers submitting the same nonce concurrently results in one success and one revert. The `expiry` field (wallets typically set¬Ý‚â§‚ÄØ30‚ÄØs) makes such races low‚Äëimpact, but UIs should surface the failure. ## Rationale - **Immutable logic** minimizes upgrade risk; a new version requires an explicit 7702 `0x04` call. - A **two‚Äëmodule** boundary captures common customizations without growing byte‚Äëcode. - No hard `maxTargets`; advanced users can bundle many calls, while conservative users install a size‚Äëcapping Policy module. - Domain separator binds the real `chainId` to mitigate cross‚Äëchain replays. ## Reference Implementation Reference implementation can be found here [`MASW.sol`](../assets/eip-7988/MASW.sol). ## Security Considerations | Threat | Mitigation | | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Same‚Äëchain replay | Global `metaNonce` | | Cross‚Äëchain replay | Chain‚Äëbound domain separator | | Fee grief / over‚Äëcharge | Fee is part of signed data; front‚Äërunning risk sits with relayer | | Batch gas grief | Optional Policy can reject oversized batches | | `ERC20` non‚Äëstandard returns | OpenZeppelin `SafeERC20` transfer check | | Re‚Äëentrancy | `nonReentrant` guard; state mutated only before external calls (nonce++) and after (fee transfer) | | Malicious Module | Core logic immutable; swapping modules needs an owner‚Äësigned tx | ## Copyright Copyright and related rights waived via [CC0](../LICENSE.md).
